content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 · · · 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance · · Administration · · Executive/Legal · · Enforcement
(916) 322-5662        322-5660              322-5901              323-6441

October 15, 1984


Harold R. Densmore
City Attorney
City of Patterson
P.O. Box 51
Newman, CA  95360

Re:  Advice Letter No. A-84-247

Dear Mr. Densmore:

     Thank you for your request for additional advice on the
conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.

QUESTION PRESENTED

     If, under the provisions of the Political Reform Act, a city
councilman must disqualify himself on a governmental decision,
must the disqualification be accompanied by an announcement of
the reason for the disqualification?

DISCUSSION

     When a city councilman decides to abstain from participating
in a decision because of his financial interests, the official's
determination must be accompanied by the disclosure of the
financial interest. This disclosure must be made part of the
agency's official record. (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section
18700(b)(5).) It is sufficient for a city councilman to enter
into the public record a statement that he is disqualifying
himself on a decision because it is reasonably foreseeable that
the decision will have a material financial effect on an
"investment interest," "interest in real property," "source of
income," or on a "business entity which he holds a business
position."

SUPERSEDED
BY A-98-239(Bennett)

ADVICE FILE SUMMARY SHEET

TYPE A          STAFF [illegible]

YEAR 07         ADVISEE Bennett

NO. 239         REQUESTOR [illegible]

DATE 10|29      AGENCY Redwood City

SUBJECT:
___ c/i                    ___ c/i code              ___ honoraria
___ campaign              ___ lobbying              ___ § 8408
___ sei                   ___ revolving door        ___ gift limits        ___ personal
___ mass mail             ___ co-sponsored         ___ prop 208           ___ use
                          events                                          ___ misc

COMPUTER ENTRY SUMMARY: (no more than 5 typed lines for summary)
The executive director of a nonprofit has a conflict
in divisions to grant funds to the nonprofit. The
public generally exception applies to decisions
affectin entities competing for the same funds.
This letter also discusses the "nexus test"

CODE §§ AND REGULATIONS: §7100, §7103, 18705 3  18707.4

BULLETIN SUMMARY: (for more space, write on back)
Disclosure of interest dimming disqualification
must be made with the same level of specificity
* at least equal to the disclosure listed on the
low officials statement of economic interests

SUPERSEDES: (Note if this advice letter supersedes others in whole or in part)
Hilthon 85-043

Dersmacr 84-247

* Bulletin = (Computer + Bulletin)

Harold R. Densmore
October 15, 1984
Page 2


     I hope that I have answered your question for you.  If I can
be of any additional help, please feel free to contact me at
(916) 322-5901.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        Janis Shank McLean
                                        Staff Counsel
                                        Legal Division

JSM:plh
Enclosure

City of Patterson

P.O. Box 667, Patterson, California 95363 (209) 892-2041

September 21, 1984


Ms. Janis Shank McLean
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. McLean:

Re: Dr. Thomas Klein
     Your Advice No. A-84-152

On August 15, 1984 you mailed to Dr. Klein your opinion in the above
matter. One of the conclusions reached was that Dr. Klein must dis-
qualify himself from any decision which would foreseeably have a
material financial effect on the Medical Care Foundation or the Dental
Foundation, although you also concluded he could approve
payments to the Medical Care Foundation as long as that act was purely
ministerial.

It is my understanding that the part of the disqualification process
is an announcement that the official is disqualifying himself or her-
self from a governmental decision because of a conflict of interest,
the reason for the disqualification and the financial interest which
may be affected by the decision. Dr. Klein has been abstaining from
voting on approval of warrants to the Medical Care Foundation. However,
he has not, so far as I am aware, announced his disqualification or
the reasons for the disqualification or the financial interest which
may be affected by the decision. If this is a strict compliance with
the rules for disqualification and abstention, should it not be
brought to his attention, and if this is so is it proper that your
office do this as a follow up to your opinion of August 15, 1984, or
should it be done through our office.

Thank you for your advice.

                                        Yours very truly,


                                        HAROLD R. DENSMORE,
                                        City Attorney
                                        P. O. Box 51
                                        Newman, CA 95360

HRD:cc

cc: Henry G. Hesling, City Manager
cc: Mayor Pat D. Maisetti

sections.question: If, under the provisions of the Political Reform Act, a city councilman must disqualify himself on a governmental decision, must the disqualification be accompanied by an announcement of the reason for the disqualification?
sections.analysis:
When a city councilman decides to abstain from participating in a decision because of his financial interests, the official's determination must be accompanied by the disclosure of the financial interest. This disclosure must be made part of the agency's official record. (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(b)(5).) It is sufficient for a city councilman to enter into the public record a statement that he is disqualifying himself on a decision because it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on an "investment interest," "interest in real property," "source of income," or on a "business entity which he holds a business position."

ADVICE FILE SUMMARY SHEET

TYPE A
YEAR 92
NO. 237
DATE 10/29

STAFF Julia
ADVISEE Bennett
REQUESTOR Lipp
AGENCY Redwood City

SUBJECT:
__ c/i
__ campaign
__ sei
__ mass mail
__ c/i code
__ lobbying
__ revolving door
__ co-sponsored
__ events
__ honoraria
__ § 84308
__ gift limits
__ prop 208
__ personal use
__ misc

COMPUTER ENTRY SUMMARY: (no more than 5 typed lines for summary)
The executive director of a nonprofit has a conflict in decisions to grant funds to the nonprofit. The public generally exception applies to decisions affecting entities competing for the same funds. This letter also discusses the "nexus test."

CODE §§ AND REGULATIONS: 87100, 87103, 18705 3, 18707.4

BULLETIN SUMMARY: (for more space, write on back)
Disclosure of interest prompting disqualification must be made with the same level of specificity at least equal to the disclosure listed on the official's statement of economic interests.

SUPERSEDES: (Note if this advice letter supersedes others in whole or in part)
Hutton 85-043
Densmore 84-247

* Bulletin = Computer + Bulletin

Harold R. Densmore  
October 15, 1984  

I hope that I have answered your question for you. If I can be of any additional help, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.
